disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single intensely red, hot, exquisitely painful joint and no fever/rigors (against septic arthritis)
trigger_question_examples: Is any ONE joint bright red, hot, and agony to even move slightly?
Any fever, shakes, or feeling generally unwell?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No single joint is red or hot, it's both hands equally, and I have no fever.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral symmetric MCP/PIP ache after overuse in known seronegative RA; passive movement not agonising
trigger_question_examples: Did this follow a day of heavy hand use, and is it symmetric across both hands?
Do you have a diagnosed inflammatory arthritis?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, after a heavy hand-work day, both hands equally, and I have mild seronegative RA.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I going to be permanently crippled? Should I go to hospital?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Reassure that a symmetric overuse flare of mild seronegative RA rarely causes rapid destruction, screen for a single hot joint/fever (septic arthritis) which is the escalation trigger, and if absent, offer NSAIDs, warmth and rest with a septic-arthritis safety-net — without ED over-triage.
